markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Restricted">
[Mantle of the Melting Heart](/Spells.aspx?ID=1373)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 143

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You bathe yourself in a mantle of liquid metal, gilding your skin and armor with an oily sheen. Upon Casting this Spell, pick two of the options below. As a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, you can change one of your chosen abilities to a different option from the list.

- **Copper Core** You draw electricity toward you, then disperse it. You gain resistance 10 to electricity. All electricity effects within 30 feet of you must succeed at a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against your spell DC or target you and only you.
- **Golden Flesh** Your body gains the chemical inertness of gold, barely reacting to substances that would otherwise greatly disturb your physiology. You become immune to [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your checks against any poison and disease effects currently affecting you.
- **Reactive Touch** Your touch reacts easily with other metals. Any metal that touches you or that you touch takes 2d6 acid damage that bypasses Hardness.
- **Weighted Grasp** Your arms become long cables, your fists heavy as anchors. You gain a cabled fist [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack with the [disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), and [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196) traits and that's in the [flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7) group. Your cabled fist deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage plus an additional 1d6 poison damage and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18).
name: Mantle of the Melting Heart

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 144 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Defense** Fortitude --- Your form ripples as you take on the fluidity and toxicity of quicksilver. You partially shift into a liquid metal form and Stride up to twice your speed. During this movement, you can pass through one creature's space—without needing to attempt a check to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29)—by splitting into droplets of mercury and reforming on the other side, though you can't end your turn in such a space. Passing through a creature's space in this way exposes it to mercury poisoning, with a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The target becomes sickened 2 and takes 6d6 poison damage.
**Critical Failure** The target becomes sickened 2 and takes 12d6 poison damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6 on a failure and 2d6 on a critical failure.
name: Mercurial Stride

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 144 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Any spells which require metal to function require the PC to be in possession of at least one chunk of that metal or an item made of that metal._ **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 60 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** AC --- You shape three needles out of a piece of metal in your possession and send them flying in a tight group toward one target. Make a spell attack roll against your target's AC. The needles deal 3d4 piercing damage and might cause bleeding. The needles impart any special properties of the metal that forms them; for instance, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) needles deal additional damage to creatures with weakness to cold iron. All the needles are made of the same metal, and the metal returns to you after the attack. **Critical Success** The target takes double damage and 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Success** The target takes full damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** You send one additional needle, increasing the regular damage by 1d4 and increasing the persistent bleed damage on a critical hit by 1.
name: Needle Darts

Any creature that has the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) trait, has the [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait, or has an anathema toward metal, takes the following effects depending on the result of that Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature suffers no additional effects.
**Success** The creature takes 1 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) as long as it takes this persistent damage.
**Failure** As success, except 1d4 persistent poison damage and sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** As success, except 1d8 persistent poison damage and sickened 3. --- **Heightened (+1)** The initial poison damage increases by 2d6.
name: Noxious Metals

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Rust Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1377)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 145

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deity**
[Ferrumnestra](/Deities.aspx?ID=688)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 20-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Red-blown flecks of rust jitter in the air, forming a cloud of agitated particles. All creatures within the cloud become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the cloud become concealed to creatures within it. The cloud deals 5d10 slashing damage to any creature that starts its turn in the area, with a basic Fortitude save.

 Metal that rusts off of a creature adds to the cloud. When a [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) creature takes damage from the cloud, the cloud's area increases by 5 feet (to a maximum of a 40-foot burst), and the creature starts to rust, taking 1d4 [persistent slashing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) damage. You can Dismiss the cloud.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The cloud's slashing damage increases by 1d10 and the persistent damage increases by 1d4.
name: Rust Cloud

[Shielded Arm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1378)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 145

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute or until expended
</row>

</column>

---

Reinforcing veins of ore run through the target's arm, letting it ward off blows with its bare skin. It can use the [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98) action to instead raise its arm, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC. It can [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) with its Raised arm as well; when it does, the target reduces the damage as if it had a shield with Hardness 4 and 15 Hit Points. This shield doesn't have a Broken Threshold, and the spell ends if the shield's Hit Points are expended.

 This spell doesn't modify the target's [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks and can't be used to make a [shield bash](/Weapons.aspx?ID=40) Strike. Casting or coming under the effects of this spell also counts as using a metallic item with regards to anathema.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The Hardness increases by 4, and the Hit Points increase by 15.
name: Shielded Arm

[Wall of Metal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1379)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 145

**Traditions**

**Deity**
[Ptah](/Deities.aspx?ID=638)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You call forth a sheet of metal, forming a wall of [iron](/Rules.aspx?ID=730), low-grade [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272), or standard-grade [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277) up to 60 feet long, 30 feet high, and 1/4 inch thick. The wall doesn't need to stand vertically but must form a straight line in an unbroken open space so it doesn't pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost. Each 10-foot-by-10- foot section of the wall has AC 10 and is immune to critical hits and precision damage. The wall's Hardness, HP, and BT use the statistics of a structure of the material you chose. Creatures can move through a section of the wall that's broken.

---
**Heightened (7th)** The wall is high-grade silver or standard-grade [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275).

**Heightened (8th)** The wall is standard-grade cold iron.

**Heightened (9th)** The wall is high-grade dawnsilver.

**Heightened (10th)** The wall is high-grade cold iron or standard-grade [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271).
name: Wall of Metal

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Buoyant Bubbles](/Spells.aspx?ID=1383)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 172

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a thin layer of foamy bubbles that adhere to the target, causing it to float in water and similar liquids. The target doesn't sink, even if it hasn't succeeded at a [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) check this round; an already-sinking target resurfaces with the bubbles' help over the course of 1 round. If on a plane where the water or liquid has a surface, the bubbles also prevent the target from diving beneath that surface unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save against your spell DC.

---
**Heightened (4th)** You can target up to 5 creatures.
name: Buoyant Bubbles

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 172 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deity** [Ytildos](/Deities.aspx?ID=706) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Reflex **Duration** 1 minute --- Barnacles, coral, and other rough aquatic creatures grow on the target's body, getting in between their joints and stiffening their movement. This impedes the target's movement depending on the result of its saving throw. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3). The target can Interact to scrape the coral growths from its body, decreasing the clumsy condition to 0 and ending the spell.
**Failure** The target is clumsy 2. Each round at the beginning of its turn, the target becomes clumsy 1 or increases its clumsy condition by 1, to a maximum of 4. The target can Interact to scrape the coral growths from its body, decreasing the clumsy condition to 0 and ending the spell. If the target's clumsy condition caused by _coral scourge_ reaches 4, the growths spread to cover the target's entire body, and the target becomes [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but when the target Interacts to scrape the coral growths from its body, it reduces its clumsy condition by 1, instead of decreasing it to 0, and decreasing the clumsy condition to 0 doesn't end the spell.
name: Coral Scourge

<title level="1" right="Spell 7" pfs="Standard">
[Dancing Fountain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1385)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 172

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 30-foot burst centered on you

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Tapping the ground, you create a shallow pool of water that spreads over the affected area, and majestic sprays, jets, and mists of water erupt forth in a dazzling water show. When you Cast the Spell, and the first time each round you Sustain the spell, you can command the fountain to use one of the following displays, which is accompanied by a loud burst of music. The _dancing fountain_ is somewhat fickle in its desire for novelty, meaning it can't be commanded to use the same display on two consecutive turns.

- **Chasing Jets** A series of vertical jets shoot up in a chase sequence, forcing one creature within the fountain to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 15 feet in a direction of your choice unless it succeeds at a Reflex save against your spell DC. The fountain can't make a creature move outside its area.
- **Flashing Spray** Diffuse fog fills the area of the emanation. All creatures within the fountain become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the fountain become concealed to creatures within it. The spray persists until the beginning of your next turn.
- **Power Rings** The fountain blasts a series of rings of water into the air that land with a crash in a 10-foot burst centered on a location of your choice within the fountain. The falling torrent deals 10d6 bludgeoning damage to all creatures within the burst ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex).
name: Dancing Fountain

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 172

[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

</row>

</column>

---

You make the briefest of dives through the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7). You [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) 10 feet, and where you would land on the ground, you instead disappear in a splash of water, your body entering the surface like a diver entering a pool. You then exit from an unoccupied surface within 40 feet, appearing in another splash of water as you Leap out, landing 10 feet away from where you exited. Each splash deals 3d10 bludgeoning damage to all creatures in a 5-foot emanation; creatures in multiple splash zones take damage only once.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage dealt by each splash increases by 1d10.
name: Dive and Breach

<actions string="Two Actions" />

<traits>

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 10 feet

**Target** 1 object up to 10 Bulk
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You draw water out of an object, possibly to clean up spills or quickly dry a soaked book or shirt. You draw up to a pint of water from the object; this dries objects of less than 1 Bulk. The water collects in a globule floating in your hand, which you can direct into a nearby container as part of Casting the Spell; otherwise, it splashes to the ground. Repeated applications of _draw moisture_ can be used to dry larger objects, although doing so might take significant time. You can use this spell in especially humid environments to condense drinkable water from the air, though typically, you can't draw more than a few cups before depleting the ambient moisture.
name: Draw Moisture

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 173 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 120 feet **Area** 20-foot burst **Defense** Reflex (see text) **Duration** sustained up to 10 minutes --- Intense cold rain comes from nowhere, a microcosm of a sudden downpour, and a magical tweak can turn the rain to freezing sleet. The driving rain and pooling water make the area [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and extinguish non-magical fires. On subsequent rounds, the first time you Sustain the spell each round, you can move the area up to 20 feet and can also freeze the rain. If you freeze the rain, each creature in the area takes 4d6 cold damage and might be [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), depending on result of its Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is slowed 1 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is slowed 2 for 1 round. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6.
name: Freezing Rain

<actions string="Two Actions" />

<traits>
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

---

Ice crystallizes around a creature, imprisoning it within an icy pillar. On a successful saving throw, the creature is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) to an adjacent space of its choice; otherwise, it's frozen inside the pillar and becomes [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) as its body can barely move within the ice. The ice has AC 10, Hardness 10, and 60 Hit Point; it's immune to critical hits, cold damage, and precision damage, and it has weakness 15 to fire. If the ice is destroyed, the creature within is freed and the spell immediately ends.
name: Frost Pillar

The target takes 6d6 bludgeoning damage and other effects, depending on its Will saving throw. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.

<title level="1" right="Spell 7" pfs="Standard">
[Hungry Depths](/Spells.aspx?ID=1391)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 173

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 5-foot burst (see text)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

A spiral of dark, corrupted water appears in the ground, plunging open a door to the depths that seems to descend for miles. Disembodied glowing eyes and gnashing teeth spin within the vortex, consuming anything that crosses their path. 

All creatures in the area take 4d8 piercing damage plus 4d4 void damage (basic Reflex save). Each time you Sustain the spell, you can increase the vortex's radius by 5 feet, to a maximum of 15 feet, or you can move the vortex up to 10 feet in a straight line. Each creature the vortex moves through takes the damage with a basic Reflex save.A creature can take damage from _hungry depths_ only once per round. 

 If cast underwater, _hungry depths_ instead fills a 40-foot-tall cylinder with a 5-foot radius.

---
**Heightened (9th)** The vortex deals 5d8 piercing damage and 5d4 void damage.
name: Hungry Depths

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Misty Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1392)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 173

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Target** 1 body of water of at least 5 square feet
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

Some say water holds memories or impressions of the past, and you can raise those memories to the surface to glimpse recent events. When you Cast the Spell, you fix your mind on an amount of time in the past, up to 24 hours ago. A mist rises from the target body of water, filling a 30-foot burst and coalescing into a misty scene of the events that took place at that time within 60 feet of the water.

  Any creature who can see the area can observe the images produced, which are a translucent white color and detailed enough to show a silhouette, outline, or contour of creatures and objects that passed through the area and the motions they took. Fine details, such as facial features or written letters, are too precise for the mist to form, and the scene is silent.

 You can Sustain the spell to cause the mist to play events backward or forward, with each minute spent Sustaining corresponding to a minute of playback.

 Strong winds from a magical source can disrupt this spell if the effect succeeds at a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against your spell DC.
name: Misty Memory

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Personal Ocean](/Spells.aspx?ID=1393)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 174

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You surround yourself in a bubble-like shroud of seawater that fills your space completely and moves with you. You can breathe, speak, and perceive normally while within your _personal ocean_, and you gain a swim Speed equal to your land Speed if you don't have one. The rules of [aquatic combat](/Rules.aspx?ID=467) apply to you, attacks targeting you, or attacks passing through your square. For instance, a bludgeoning or slashing melee attack targeting you would take a –2 circumstance penalty, and you can't cast fire spells or use actions with the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) trait.

 Each time you move, you extinguish non-magical fires in spaces you pass through and can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) magical fires you move through. If you successfully counteract a fire larger than the spaces you moved through, you merely push it out of the spaces along your path. If you fail to counteract a given fire, you can't counteract that fire for the duration of the spell.

 You can Dismiss the spell.
name: Personal Ocean

<traits>

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Pillar of Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1394)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 174, [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 77

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a large, self-contained cylinder of still, clear fresh water. You choose the radius and height of the pillar, to a maximum radius of 15 feet and a maximum height of 60 feet. Creatures inside the pillar can [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) through the water as normal ([Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) DC 10), or if the spell was cast on solid ground, walk along the bottom (which is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453)). Any creature that exits the pillar falls or lands accordingly.
name: Pillar of Water

[Rousing Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1395)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 174

[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You cause a splash of cold water to descend on an ally's head, granting some temporary vigor. The target gains 1d4 temporary Hit Points, which last for 1 minute. The target is then temporarily immune to the temporary Hit Points from _rousing splash_ for 10 minutes. The target can also attempt an immediate flat check to recover from a single source of [persistent acid or fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), with the DC reduction from appropriate assistance.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The amount of temporary Hit Points granted increases by 1d4.
name: Rousing Splash

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Scrying Ripples](/Spells.aspx?ID=1396)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 175

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 gallon of water or more
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You touch the water's surface, and as the ripples spread out, so do your senses. When you Cast this Spell, you automatically know the location of any unoccupied, flowing water with a surface area of at least 1 square within 500 feet. You can pick any one of these streams to see and hear out of for the duration of the spell, though your field of vision is perpendicular to the flowing surface. For example, you could look normally out of a waterfall but would look straight up from out of a river. Each time you Sustain the spell, you can change to a different water source or move elsewhere along the same one.

 If the water you're scrying through ceases flowing, such as by a faucet turning off, a river damming, or a waterfall freezing over, this spell ends. Additionally, your face appears as a faint reflection in the water's surface as you peek through. A creature that [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) and succeeds at their Perception check against your spell DC detects your presence in the water.

---
**Heightened (5th)** You know the location of and can scry through flowing water within 1 mile.
name: Scrying Ripples

The hydrophobic properties of the spell also grant resistance 10 to acid and a +2 bonus to saves and DCs against damaging effects with the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) or [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) traits.

**Heightened (4th)** The duration is 1 day.

**Heightened (8th)** The duration is unlimited.

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 175 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deity** [Ytildos](/Deities.aspx?ID=706) **Range** 500 feet **Area** 25-foot radius, 20-foot tall cylinder **Defense** Reflex **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- With a spin of your hand, you create a large swirling vortex of storming waves. All squares in the _whirlpool_ are difficult terrain. The constant churning of the fierce waves means that creatures are constantly tossed between areas of air and water, allowing both air- and water-breathing creatures to breathe within the vortex. Any creature with a swim Speed can [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) instead of Stride to move within the _whirlpool_; the DC to Swim within it is equal to your spell DC. All creatures in the area take 6d10 bludgeoning damage as the waves crash into them. On subsequent rounds, the first time you Sustain the spell each round, the waves crash again with the same effect. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [drawn](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet toward the center of the _whirlpool_.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is drawn to the center of the _whirlpool_. --- **Heightened (+1)** The bludgeoning damage increases by 1d10.
name: Whirlpool

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Entwined Roots](/Spells.aspx?ID=1400)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 196

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 20-foot burst

**Target** up to 5 willing living creatures
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

Slithering roots gird you and your allies in layers of flexible wooden protection. Each affected creature gains resistance 5 to bludgeoning and piercing damage. Whenever a creature protected by these roots is targeted by a ranged weapon attack but takes no damage (such as if the attack missed or the damage was reduced to 0 by resistance), the roots snatch up the ammunition or thrown weapon and hold it. The protected creature can retrieve the thrown weapon or ammunition as an Interact action.

---
**Heightened (7th)** The resistances increase to 10.

**Heightened (9th)** The resistances increase to 15.
name: Entwined Roots

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Flourishing Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1401)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 196

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deities**
[Blooms of the Spreading Weald](/Deities.aspx?ID=365), [Khepri](/Deities.aspx?ID=635), [Zibik](/Deities.aspx?ID=685)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 5-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

</row>

</column>

---

Plants rapidly grow up from the ground. All creatures in the target area take 2d4 damage. The type of damage depends on the type of plant you choose to grow. On a critical failure, targets experience additional effects, also depending on what you choose to grow. The type of plant and its effects are chosen when you Cast the Spell.

- **Cacti** Piercing damage, and 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) on a critical failure.
- **Flowers** Poison damage, and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 2 rounds on a critical failure.
- **Fruits** Bludgeoning damage, and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 2 rounds on a critical failure.
- **Roots** Bludgeoning damage, and the affected creatures fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) on a critical failure.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4, and the persistent bleed damage from cacti increases by 1.
name: Flourishing Flora

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Helpful Wood Spirits](/Spells.aspx?ID=1402)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 196

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 5-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Upon casting this spell, a cluster of little wood spirits tumbles into the area. They perform a simple task each round. These spirits are a magical echo of the true spirits that live on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) and can't be attacked, damaged, or otherwise interfered with. These spirits often look like [kodama](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1202) but can appear in any of the countless varieties of wood spirits that inhabit the Plane of Wood.

 Choose the kind of aid your wood spirits supply from the list below when you Cast the Spell. The first time each round you Sustain the spell, you can move the area occupied by the apparitions up to 10 feet and you can choose the kind of aid they provide this round.

- **Chores** The wood spirits perform a minor manual task of your choice, such as cleaning, opening a door, picking up an unattended object, or carrying an object from one location to another. The spirits can't pick up or carry an object of greater than 2 Bulk.
- **Distract** The wood spirits distract your foes, clambering all over their feet and bodies and being a nuisance. Creatures in the area are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) unless they succeed at a Reflex save.
- **Obstacle** The wood spirits fill the area as a chaotic, bouncing mass. The area they occupy is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).
- **Search** The wood spirits [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) in the area they occupy, using your Perception check as their own. You learn anything they do from Seeking in this way.
name: Helpful Wood Spirits

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 197 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 120 feet **Target** 1 creature made of flesh **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** varies --- You turn the target's flesh slowly into wood. The target must attempt a Fortitude save. Creatures with the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) trait have a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against this spell. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is slowed 1 and must attempt a Fortitude save at the end of each of its turns; this ongoing save has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait. On a failed save, the slowed condition increases by 1 (or 2 on a critical failure). A successful save reduces the slowed condition by 1. When a creature is unable to act due to the slowed condition from _lignify_, the creature is permanently non-magically [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), though it's turned to wood instead of stone. The spell ends if the creature is magically petrified or the slowed condition is removed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is initially slowed 2.
name: Lignify

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Lotus Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=1405)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 197

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Lotuses, water lilies, and other watery plants bloom at your feet, allowing you to walk on the surface of water and other liquids. You can go underwater if you wish, but in that case, you must [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) normally. As you Stride or Step on water, you leave a trail of oversized lily pads, lotus leaves, or spatterdock that fill the squares' surfaces in your wake. Each square of this trail can withstand the weight of 1 Medium creature and lasts until the end of your next turn. You can Dismiss this spell early.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The duration of this spell increases to 10 minutes. The trail lasts until the spell ends or you Dismiss the spell on you, whichever comes first.

**Heightened (6th)** The duration increases to 1 hour. Your trail is permanent even if you Dismiss the spell on you, although if the water can't support such plants, they'll die in a week. The trail can be removed by [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) or a similar spell.
name: Lotus Walk

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Mantle of the Unwavering Heart](/Spells.aspx?ID=1406)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 197

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Vines and branches envelop you in an instant, growing a wild bloom of colors that open to reveal your new form. Upon Casting this Spell, pick two of the options below. As a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, you can change one of your chosen abilities to a different option from the list.

- **Evergreen Vitality** You gain [fast healing 3](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) and a +2 bonus to saves against [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46). This effect has the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) and [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) traits.
- **Overwhelming Perfume** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) A cloud of potent floral scent surrounds you in a 15-foot emanation. Creatures in the aura can't benefit from the [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) ability and are [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) as long as they remain in the aura. The sickness is a disease effect.
- **Towering Trunk** You grow to a considerable height. Increase your size to Large. You're [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3). Your [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) increases by 5 feet (or by 10 feet if you started out Tiny), and you gain a grasping branch unarmed attack; this attack is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) weapon group and deals 2d8 bludgeoning damage plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18).
- **Unyielding Will** Your mind becomes incredibly resilient to change. You're immune to being [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), and you get a +1 bonus to saves and DCs against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects.
name: Mantle of the Unwavering Heart

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 197 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** 100 feet **Area** four unoccupied 5-foot squares, each of which is at least 20 feet apart **Duration** 12 hours --- You cultivate four wooden bulbs, each filled with toxic pollen and sensitive to motion. When a creature enters a space adjacent to a bulb, or when a bulb is touched or damaged (each bulb has AC 5), the bulb blossoms and releases pollen in a 15-foot emanation. Creatures in the area take 8d8 poison damage and must attempt a Fortitude save with the following results. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage, becomes dazzled for 1 round, and becomes [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is dazzled for 1 round, and becomes stupefied 2 for 1 minute. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d8.
name: Pollen Pods

</row>

</row>

---

You gain general insights into the immediate vicinity by sending your sense through the root systems of trees and bushes. If there are any [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41) Medium or larger creatures in the area, you learn of their presence and they're [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) to you. You can immediately attempt to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) an undetected creature, and you gain a +1 status bonus to this attempt and any of your other attempts to Seek [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or undetected creatures until the end of your next turn. You also learn if any such creatures passed through this area in the last hour, although you get only the vaguest sense of direction from the spell. If you begin to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) a creature detected in this way, you gain a +1 status bonus to the initial check.
name: Root Reading

The splinters deal double damage on a critical hit and 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). You can spend a third action while casting this spell to fire splinters at two different targets instead of one. These attacks each increase your multiple attack penalty, but you don't increase your multiple attack penalty until after you make both spell attack rolls for _splinter volley_.

---
**Heightened (+2)** Increase the damage dealt to each target by 4d6 and the persistent bleed damage by 1.

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Weave Wood](/Spells.aspx?ID=1415)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 199

**Traditions**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4), [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)

**Deity**
[Shumunue](/Deities.aspx?ID=695)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 piece of unprocessed wood, reeds, or plant fiber of 1 Bulk or less
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

With a touch, you cause the target to break into fibrous strands that then weave themselves into a woven mundane object of the same Bulk or less, such as a basket, hat, shield, or mat. You can create up to 4 objects with one casting of this spell, providing their total Bulk doesn't exceed the Bulk of your target. The objects have Hardness 2 and 8 Hit Points.

---
**Heightened (+1)** Increase the maximum Bulk that you can target by 1 and the maximum number of objects you can create by 2.
name: Weave Wood

[Wooden Double](/Spells.aspx?ID=1416)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 199

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** You're critically hit by a damage-dealing effect or Strike

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A wooden double appears out of nowhere and takes the blow in your place. Take a Step action. A wooden block of your size and roughly shaped like you appears in the space you left and absorbs the hit. This block has Hardness 5 and 20 Hit Points. If the wooden block is destroyed, you take any excess damage that the block didn't absorb. After taking the blow, the wooden block collapses into a pile of splinters and dust.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The block's Hit Points increase by 10.
name: Wooden Double

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Wooden Fists](/Spells.aspx?ID=1417)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 199

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Your arms and hands swell with new growth, transforming into tree trunks twice as big as their current size. Your fists deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage, lose the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait, and have [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192).

---
**Heightened (3rd)** Your fists gain the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) trait and become a [_striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) weapon, increasing the damage your fists deal to 2d6 bludgeoning.

**Heightened (7th)** Your fists gain the magical trait and become a _greater striking_ weapon, increasing the damage your fists deal to 3d6 bludgeoning. The duration is 10 minutes.

**Heightened (9th)** Your fists gain the magical trait and become a _major striking_ weapon, increasing the damage your fists deal to 4d6 bludgeoning. The duration is 1 hour.
name: Wooden Fists

All wood, dead or alive, is your armory. When you cast this spell, you draw forth a _+1_ weapon of your choice from the target. The weapon must be 2 Bulk or less. This weapon is of the same material as the target.

**Heightened (3rd)** The weapon is a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ weapon.

**Heightened (5th)** The weapon is a _+2 striking_ weapon.

**Heightened (7th)** The weapon is a _+3 greater striking_ weapon.

<title level="1" right="Focus 4" pfs="Standard">
[Wood Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=1419)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 199

**Domain**
[Wood](/Domains.aspx?ID=63)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Wood shapes itself according to your will readily, making staircases appear as you climb up the side of a tree, automatically weaving vines into ladders, or lowering branches to lift you up to the top. Move up to your Speed in any direction or along any surface, so long as there are living plants or trees of size Large or larger within 15 feet of you. The wood allows you to move but doesn't protect you from [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454) or other dangers.

 Each time you Sustain this spell, you can move again in the same way.

---
**Heightened (8th)** The duration of this spell becomes 10 minutes. You no longer need to Sustain the spell to extend its duration, but you can Sustain it to move as described.
name: Wood Walk

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 222 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Spell Lists** [Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5) **Area** 60-foot cone **Defense** [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex --- You open a miniature portal to an elemental plane within your mouth, then exhale a powerful explosion of the plane's element. Roll 1d6 to determine the element. If you used 3 actions to Cast this Spell, you can choose the element instead. This spell gains the trait matching the element. Each creature in the cone must attempt a basic Reflex save.
  1. **Air** Strong winds and lightning strikes buffet creatures in the cone, dealing 6d10 electricity damage. A creature that fails its save is also [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet.
  2. **Earth** A cone of mud deals 5d10 bludgeoning damage to creatures in the area. A creature on the ground that fails its save slips and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
  3. **Fire** A surging cone of fire deals 7d10 fire damage.
  4. **Metal** A cone of rust slices flesh and damages metal. Each creature in the area takes 5d10 slashing damage. A creature made of metal gets a result one degree of success worse than it rolled, and an unattended metal object gets a critical failure.
  5. **Water** A torrent of chilled water deals 3d10 cold damage and 3d10 bludgeoning damage.
  6. **Wood** Branches rip through creatures, dealing 3d10 piercing damage and 3d10 slashing damage. Ground in the area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for 1 minute.
--- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d10. For water and wood, you can choose which damage type increases for each rank the spell is heightened.
name: Elemental Breath

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Elemental Counter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1421)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 222

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Spell Lists**
[Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5)

<row gap="medium">

 

**Trigger** You or a creature within 60 feet rolls a saving throw against a spell with the [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait, or are targeted by a spell attack with such a trait.

**Requirements** You have a spell slot from which you could Cast a Spell of the triggering spell's countering element; see text.
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Each element in the elemental cycle counters another, and you can use your elemental spells to protect against elements they counter. You lose your spell slot as if you had cast the triggering spell. You then attempt to counteract the triggering spell, using the rank of the spell you lost for the counteract rank. You can lose a plant or wood spell to counter earth, an earth spell to counter water, a water spell to counter fire, a fire spell to counter metal, or a metal spell to counter plant or wood.
name: Elemental Counter
requirement_markdown: You have a spell slot from which you could Cast a Spell of the triggering spell's countering element; see text.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Elemental Sense](/Spells.aspx?ID=1422)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Revelation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=138" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 222

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Spell Lists**
[Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

The elements grant you sensory enhancements. Choose one of the options below. The spell gains the listed trait or traits.

- **Aquatic Echolocation** ([water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) You use your hearing as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense when underwater.
- **Heatsense** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) You can sense creatures with a temperature of 90º Fahrenheit or higher within 30 feet as an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) sense.
- **Magnetoreception** ([metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) You can sense creatures carrying metal items with a total of 1 Bulk or greater; this is an imprecise sense with a range of 30 feet. If a creature is made of metal or has the metal trait, you can detect it as though magnetoreception were a precise sense. In addition, you continuously know which direction is north.
- **Mechanoreception** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5)) You can see [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creatures and objects. They appear to you as translucent shapes, and they are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to you.
- **Tremorsense** ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)) You gain [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) with a range of 30 feet.
- **Woodsense** ([plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) You can sense creatures carrying wood items with a total of 1 Bulk or greater; this is an imprecise sense with a range of 30 feet. If a creature is made of wood or has the plant or wood trait, you can detect it as though woodsense were a precise sense.

---
**Heightened (6th)** The duration increases to 8 hours, and you can take 1 minute to recalibrate the sense, switching from your current elemental sense to a different one.
name: Elemental Sense

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 8" pfs="Standard">
[Summon Elemental Herald](/Spells.aspx?ID=1423)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Incarnate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=388" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 222

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Spell Lists**
[Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the end of your next turn
</row>

</column>

---

Your meditation into the different elemental planes connects you to them deeply, and this connection allows you to summon a herald of an [elemental lord](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=8). The herald arrives in the form of a natural disaster centralized on your enemies. The herald occupies the space of a Huge creature. When you Cast this Spell, choose one of the elemental heralds below to summon; if you're a divine spellcaster who worships an elemental lord, you must choose the option matching your deity's element. This spell gains the trait ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) matching your choice.

- **Air** Speed fly 60 feet; **Arrive** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5)) _Supercell Front_ The herald of air arrives in the form of a twisting tornado, with high-speed winds slamming into your enemies. Each enemy in a 100-foot emanation takes 10d8 bludgeoning damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save; **Depart** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5)) _Twister Downdraft_ Each enemy in a 60-foot line must attempt a Fortitude save. A creature that fails is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) if it's on the ground or descends 40 feet if it's in the air. A creature that descends takes [falling damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) if this forced movement brings it to the ground.
- **Earth** Speed 50 feet, burrow 30 feet; **Arrive** ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)) _Tectonic Rise_ The herald of earth erupts from the ground, splitting it open in a 50-foot emanation. Each enemy in the emanation takes 10d6 slashing damage from the flying debris with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. Enemies who critically fail [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) 30 feet into the crevasse, taking falling damage (the sides of the fissure require successful DC 15 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33)); **Depart** ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) _Plate Crash_ The herald of earth sinks back into the ground, and the sides of the crevasse slam together with a deafening blow, dealing 5d10 sonic damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save to all enemies in a 30-foot emanation. An enemy that critically fails is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 10 minutes.
- **Fire** Speed 60 feet, fly 40 feet; **Arrive** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) _Magma Flow_ The herald of fire flows into the area as living magma, dealing 10d6 fire damage and 3d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to all enemies in a 60-foot emanation with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save; **Depart** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) _Flame Vortex_ Each enemy in a 60-foot line takes 6d6 fire damage and 3d6 persistent fire damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save as the fire herald whips into a fire pillar before vanishing.
- **Metal** Speed 50 feet, fly 50 feet; **Arrive** ([metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) _Shard Cloud_ The herald of metal arrives as a cloud of metal shards and static electricity, dealing 4d8 slashing damage and 4d12 electricity damage to all enemies in a 50-foot emanation with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save; **Depart** ([metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) _Rust Mist_ The shards in the cloud rapidly rust and cling to enemies in the 50-foot emanation, dealing 10d6 damage to metal creatures and objects in the area with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save, ignoring Hardness. Unattended objects automatically critically fail this save.
- **Water** Speed 40 feet, swim 60 feet; **Arrive** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) _Hurricane Formation_ The herald of water forms as a miniature hurricane. Each enemy in a 60-foot emanation takes 4d10 bludgeoning damage from the slamming rains and 4d10 cold damage from the falling hail with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save; **Depart** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) _Cataclysmic Deluge_ The hurricane collapses into a rushing flood, dealing 6d8 bludgeoning damage to all enemies in a 120-foot cone with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 20 feet away from the herald (or 40 feet on a critical failure).
- **Wood** Speed 50 feet, burrow 30 feet; **Arrive** ([plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) _Advantageous Roots_ The herald of wood bursts forth from the ground as a complex root system. Each enemy in a 50-foot emanation takes 6d10 piercing damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save as the roots skewer its limbs and body. Each enemy that fails its save takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the herald departs; **Depart** ([plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) _Retreat to Soil_ The roots retreat into the ground, dealing 6d10 piercing damage to each enemy in a 50-foot emanation with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. Any creature with a penalty to Speeds from its initial save automatically takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The ground in the emanation is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) after the roots retreat.
name: Summon Elemental Herald

<title level="1" right="Focus 3" pfs="">
[Ki Cutting Sight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1424)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=112" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Wake the Dead #2](/Sources.aspx?ID=207) pg. 29

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Ki flows through everything in existence—animals and plants, living and dead, mortals and spirits. Strike at just the right place, in just the right way, and the flow is stopped with dire consequences.

When you Cast the Spell, make a Perception check against the target's Fortitude DC. If you succeed, the next time you deal damage to the target with a Strike or ki spell, you deal 4d10 additional slashing damage.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The precision damage increases by 1d10
name: Ki Cutting Sight

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Limited">
[Allfood](/Spells.aspx?ID=1425)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 79

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute ([somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283),  [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284))

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 unattended non-magical object of 1 Bulk or less
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You transform one object into an edible substance that any living creature can chew, swallow, and safely digest. This _allfood_ always has a bland taste and slightly gooey consistency, regardless of its original nature. One bulk of _allfood_ provides enough sustenance to feed one Medium creature for a day.

 After 1 day, if no one has eaten the _allfood_, it reverts to its original form. Most Small creatures eat one-quarter as much as a Medium creature and most Tiny creatures eat one-sixteenth as much, while most Large creatures eat 10 times as much, Huge creatures need 100 times as much, and so on.

---
**Heightened (+1)** Double the maximum bulk (to a maximum of 256 bulk at 10th level).
name: Allfood

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="">
[Deep Sight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1426)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 79

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

Your eyes darken until they become entirely pitch black. You grant yourself the ability to see in the darkness. You gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415). If you already have darkvision, you gain greater darkvision.

---
**Heightened (7th)** The duration is until the next time you make daily preparations.
name: Deep Sight

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="">
[Fate's Travels](/Spells.aspx?ID=1427)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 79

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute ([material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284))

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 corpse
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
Will

</row>

</column>

---

You get a vision of the creature when it was alive, and its last 10 minutes of travel. This vision gives you a clear impression of the route it took and locations it visited, if any, but not any creatures or hazards in those areas. This information is enough to automatically succeed at [Tracking](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) the creature over that distance. 

---
**Heightened (6th)** Your vision covers the creature's last hour of travel, and you gain an impression of the hazards, creature types, and number of creatures it encountered along the way, as well as a clear impression of how it died.
name: Fate's Travels
